forecast-period.text: A cold front moving across the Bight will stall over central SA on Sunday as a low pressure system develops near Kangaroo Island. The low is expected to move north over the Eyre Peninsula early next week, weakening as it moves into NSW. A trough may linger near the southern coasts before weak ridge of high pressure begins to move across SA later in the week.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Showers extending statewide, easing across the far northwest during the morning. Maximum temperatures below average. Northeast to northwesterly winds, ahead of a south to southwesterly change about the west and central districts, freshening near the coasts.

Showers in the west, reaching central parts in the evening. Possible thunderstorms about the far west. Northeast to northwesterly winds, with a south to southwesterly change moving across western districts during the evening, fresh at times near the coast.

Showers across most districts, most likely across the southern agricultural area. Isolated thunderstorms about the Eyre Peninsula and West Coast district. Maximum temperatures below average. Southwest to southeasterly winds fresh at times about the coast.

Showers across most districts, less likely across the far north. Isolated thunderstorms possible over the North East Pastoral District. Maximum temperatures below average. Southwest to southeasterly winds.

text.p: Isolated showers across the agricultural areas and southern pastoral districts. Maximum temperatures near to below average. Southwest to southeasterly winds fresh.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Wednesday: 1 to 5 mm about the pastoral districts, with isolated falls up to 15mm with thunderstorms. Elsewhere, 5 to 15 mm across the agricultural areas and west coast, increasing to 15 to 25 mm across western Eyre Peninsula.
